20.reason: Error: The problem requires that values greater than or equal to the root go to the right child, but this condition only handles strictly greater values, missing the case where item equals p->data.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  Error: The problem requires that values greater than or equal to the root go to the right child, but this condition only handles strictly greater values, missing the case where item equals p->data.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  Error: Should handle item == p->data by inserting to right subtree; change to "else" or "else if(item>=p->data)" to include duplicates.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  Condition should be else (item >= p->data) because duplicates should go to the right child according to the problem description.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The problem specifies that the right child's value should be greater than or equal to the root. This condition ignores duplicate values (item == p->data), causing them to be lost instead of being inserted into the right subtree. It should be changed to 'else' or 'else if(item>=p->data)'.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The condition misses the case where item == p->data. According to the problem description, the right child should be >= root, so duplicate values must be inserted into the right subtree. Change to 'else' or 'else if(item>=p->data)'.

8.reason: [Using a global variable 'height' causes incorrect height tracking during traversal because it is shared across all recursive calls and modified in preorder traversal, leading to wrong height values for leaf nodes.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Using a global variable 'height' causes incorrect height tracking during traversal because it is shared across all recursive calls and modified in preorder traversal, leading to wrong height values for leaf nodes.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Using a global variable 'height' leads to incorrect height tracking during traversal because it is shared across all recursive calls and not specific to each node.]

31.reason: [The height of the current node is not stored in the node itself, and the global 'height' variable does not reflect the actual depth of the current node due to flawed increment/decrement logic.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [The height of the current node is not stored in the node itself, and the global 'height' variable does not reflect the actual depth of the current node due to flawed increment/decrement logic.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [The height printed is the global 'height', which does not reflect the actual height of the current leaf node in the tree.]

35.reason: [Incrementing height before traversing children assumes both children exist, but if only one child exists, the height adjustment becomes inconsistent. Also, global state makes this approach error-prone.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Incrementing height before traversing children assumes both children exist, but if only one child exists, the height adjustment becomes inconsistent. Also, global state makes this approach error-prone.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Modifying a global 'height' during traversal causes incorrect values when backtracking or traversing right subtrees after left ones.]

38.reason: [Decrementing after both recursive calls fails to correctly backtrack height when only one subtree is traversed or when recursion paths diverge.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Decrementing after both recursive calls fails to correctly backtrack height when only one subtree is traversed or when recursion paths diverge.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [This decrement assumes a strict DFS stack-like behavior, but global state makes it error-prone especially with multiple branches.]
